title: oxalis versicolor var. flaviflora
intro: Oxalis versicolor var. flaviflora, commonly known as the yellow wood-sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) and is native to South Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of oxalic acid. The health benefits of oxalis versicolor var. flaviflora include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
